records.ety: [OE. burnischen, burnissen, burnen, OF. burnir, brunir, to make brown, polish, F. brunir, fr. F. brun brown, fr. OHG. br<?/n; cf. MHG. briunen to make brown, polish. See Brown, a.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Burnished (<?/); p. pr. & vb. n. Burnishing.]

defs: To cause to shine; to make smooth and bright; to polish; specifically, to polish by rubbing with something hard and smooth; as, to burnish brass or paper.
quotes: The frame of burnished steel, that east a glare From far, and seemed to thaw the freezing air.
Now the village windows blaze, Burnished by the setting sun.

defs: To shine forth; to brighten; to become smooth and glossy, as from swelling or filling out; hence, to grow large.
quotes: A slender poet must have time to grow, And spread and burnish as his brothers do.
My thoughts began to burnish, sprout, and swell.

defs: The effect of burnishing; gloss; brightness; luster.
